#!/bin/bash

# PostgreSQL Daily Backup Script
# Created: 2026-03-08
# Description: Automated PostgreSQL database backups with compression and retention
#
# USAGE:
#   ./pg_backup.sh                    # Run with default/env settings
#   ./pg_backup.sh --help               # Show help
#
# ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES:
#   PG_HOST       - PostgreSQL host (default: localhost)
#   PG_PORT       - PostgreSQL port (default: 5432)
#   PG_USER       - PostgreSQL user (default: postgres)
#   PG_PASSWORD   - PostgreSQL password (required unless using peer auth)
#   PG_DATABASE   - Database to backup (default: all databases)
#   BACKUP_DIR    - Backup storage directory (default: /var/backups/postgresql)
#   RETENTION_DAYS - Days to keep backups (default: 30)
#
# EXIT CODES:
#   0 - Success
#   1 - General error
#   2 - Missing required environment variables
#   3 - pg_dump not found
#   4 - Backup directory creation failed
#   5 - Database backup failed

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for output (only if terminal)
if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
    RED='\033[0;31m'
    GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    NC='\033[0m' # No Color
else
    RED=''
    GREEN=''
    YELLOW=''
    NC=''
fi

# Configuration with defaults
PG_HOST="${PG_HOST:-localhost}"
PG_PORT="${PG_PORT:-5432}"
PG_USER="${PG_USER:-postgres}"
PG_DATABASE="${PG_DATABASE:-}"  # Empty means backup all databases
BACKUP_DIR="${BACKUP_DIR:-/var/backups/postgresql}"
RETENTION_DAYS="${RETENTION_DAYS:-30}"

# Timestamp for filename
TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M")
LOG_TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

# Script directory for logs
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" &>/dev/null && pwd)"
LOG_FILE="${BACKUP_DIR}/backup_${TIMESTAMP}.log"

# ============================================================================
# Helper Functions
# ============================================================================

log() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    echo -e "[${LOG_TIMESTAMP}] [${level}] ${message}"
    
    # Also write to log file if directory exists
    if [[ -d "$BACKUP_DIR" ]]; then
        echo "[${LOG_TIMESTAMP}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
}

log_info() { log "INFO" "$@"; }
log_warn() { log "WARN" "${YELLOW}$@${NC}"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "${RED}$@${NC}"; }
log_success() { log "SUCCESS" "${GREEN}$@${NC}"; }

#!/bin/bash
#
# PostgreSQL Database Backup Script
# Filename: postgresql_backup.sh
# Description: Automates daily PostgreSQL backups with compression and rotation
# Author: Beta Manager (coding-3)
# Date: $(date +%Y-%m-%d)
#

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION - Can be overridden via environment variables
# ============================================================================

# Database connection settings
DB_NAME="${DB_NAME:-mydatabase}"          # Database name to backup
DB_USER="${DB_USER:-postgres}"            # PostgreSQL user
DB_HOST="${DB_HOST:-localhost}"           # Database host
DB_PORT="${DB_PORT:-5432}"                # Database port
DB_PASSWORD="${DB_PASSWORD:-}"            # PostgreSQL password (optional)

# Backup settings

#!/bin/bash
#
# System Information Script
# Outputs CPU model, total RAM, and disk usage
# Works on both Linux and macOS
#

# Colors for output (if terminal supports it)
if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'
    HEADER='\033[1;36m'  # Cyan bold
    LABEL='\033[1;33m'   # Yellow bold
    VALUE='\033[0;32m'   # Green
    RESET='\033[0m'
else
    BOLD=''
    HEADER=''
    LABEL=''
    VALUE=''
    RESET=''
fi

# Print formatted section header
print_header() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${HEADER}===============================================${RESET}"
    echo -e "${HEADER}$1${RESET}"
    echo -e "${HEADER}===============================================${RESET}"
}

# Print label-value pair
print_info() {
    local label="$1"
    local value="$2"
    echo -e "${LABEL}${label}:${RESET} ${VALUE}${value}${RESET}"
}

# Detect OS
OS="unknown"
if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    OS="macOS"
elif [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
    OS="Linux"
fi

print_header "SYSTEM INFORMATION"
print_info "Operating System" "$OS ($(uname -m) architecture)"
print_info "Hostname" "$(hostname)"
print_info "Date/Time" "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"

# ===============================
# CPU INFORMATION
# ===============================
print_header "CPU INFORMATION"

if [[ "$OS" == "macOS" ]]; then
    # macOS CPU detection
    cpu_model=$(sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
    cpu_cores=$(sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
    print_info "CPU Model" "$cpu_model"
    print_info "CPU Cores" "$cpu_cores"
elif [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
    # Linux CPU detection
    if command -v lscpu &> /dev/null; then
        cpu_model=$(lscpu | grep "Model name:" | sed 's/Model name:\s*//')
        cpu_cores=$(nproc)
    else
        cpu_model=$(cat /proc/cpuinfo | grep "model name" | head -1 | sed 's/.*: //')
        cpu_cores=$(grep -c ^processor /proc/cpuinfo)
    fi
    print_info "CPU Model" "${cpu_model:-Unknown}"
    print_info "CPU Cores" "$cpu_cores"
else
    print_info "CPU Model" "Unable to detect (unsupported OS)"
fi

# ===============================
# MEMORY INFORMATION
# ===============================
print_header "MEMORY INFORMATION"

if [[ "$OS" == "macOS" ]]; then
    # macOS memory detection
    total_ram_bytes=$(sysctl -n hw.memsize 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    total_ram_gb=$(echo "scale=2; $total_ram_bytes / 1024 / 1024 / 1024" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
    print_info "Total RAM" "${total_ram_gb} GB"
    
    # Memory pressure (if available)
    memory_pressure=$(vm_stat 2>/dev/null | grep "Pages free" | awk '{print $3}' | sed 's/\.//' || echo "")
    if [[ -n "$memory_pressure" ]]; then
        print_info "Free Pages" "$memory_pressure"
    fi
elif [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
    # Linux memory detection
    if command -v free &> /dev/null; then
        total_ram_kb=$(free -k | grep Mem | awk '{print $2}')
        total_ram_gb=$(echo "scale=2; $total_ram_kb / 1024 / 1024" | bc 2>/dev/null || awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $total_ram_kb/1024/1024}")
        print_info "Total RAM" "${total_ram_gb} GB"
        
        # Available memory
        available_ram_kb=$(free -k | grep Mem | awk '{print $7}')
        available_ram_gb=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $available_ram_kb/1024/1024}")
        print_info "Available RAM" "${available_ram_gb} GB"
    else
        # Fallback to /proc/meminfo
        total_ram_kb=$(grep MemTotal /proc/meminfo | awk '{print $2}')
        total_ram_gb=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $total_ram_kb/1024/1024}")
        print_info "Total RAM" "${total_ram_gb} GB"
    fi
else
    print_info "Total RAM" "Unable to detect (unsupported OS)"
fi

# ===============================
# DISK USAGE
# ===============================
print_header "DISK USAGE"

if command -v df &> /dev/null; then
    echo -e "${LABEL}Filesystem          Size  Used  Avail Use%  Mounted on${RESET}"
    echo "------------------------------------------------------"
    
    # Get disk usage for all mounted filesystems (human-readable)
    df -h | grep -E '^/dev/' | while read -r filesystem size used avail percent mount; do
        printf "%-20s %5s %5s %5s %4s  %s\n" "$filesystem" "$size" "$used" "$avail" "$percent" "$mount"
    done
    
    # Total summary
    echo ""
    if [[ "$OS" == "macOS" ]]; then
        total_size=$(df -h / | tail -1 | awk '{print $2}')
        total_used=$(df -h / | tail -1 | awk '{print $3}')
        total_avail=$(df -h / | tail -1 | awk '{print $4}')
        total_pct=$(df -h / | tail -1 | awk '{print $5}')
        print_info "Root (/)" "Size: $total_size | Used: $total_used | Free: $total_avail | $total_pct"
    else
        # Linux - show summary of main partitions
        df -h / 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | while read -r filesystem size used avail percent mount; do
            print_info "Root (/)" "Size: $size | Used: $used | Free: $avail | $percent"
        done
    fi
else
    print_info "Disk Usage" "Unable to detect (df command not found)"
fi

# Footer
echo ""
echo -e "${HEADER}===============================================${RESET}"
echo -e "${HEADER}End of System Report${RESET}"
echo -e "${HEADER}===============================================${RESET}"
echo ""

#!/bin/bash
#
# System Information Script
# Outputs CPU model, total RAM, and disk usage for macOS and Linux
#

set -euo pipefail

# Detect operating system
detect_os() {
    if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        echo "macos"
    elif [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
        echo "linux"
    else
        echo "unknown"
    fi
}

# Get CPU model name
get_cpu_model() {
    local os="$1"
    local cpu_model="Unknown"
    
    if [[ "$os" == "macos" ]]; then
        # macOS: use sysctl to get CPU brand
        if command -v sysctl &> /dev/null; then
            cpu_model=$(sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
        fi
    elif [[ "$os" == "linux" ]]; then
        # Linux: read from /proc/cpuinfo
        if [[ -f /proc/cpuinfo ]]; then
            cpu_model=$(grep "model name" /proc/cpuinfo | head -1 | sed 's/.*: //' || echo "Unknown")
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "$cpu_model"
}

# Get total RAM in GB
get_total_ram() {
    local os="$1"
    local total_ram_gb="Unknown"
    
    if [[ "$os" == "macos" ]]; then
        # macOS: use sysctl to get memory size in bytes
        if command -v sysctl &> /dev/null; then
            local ram_bytes
            ram_bytes=$(sysctl -n hw.memsize 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            # Convert bytes to GB
            total_ram_gb=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $ram_bytes/1024/1024/1024}")
        fi
    elif [[ "$os" == "linux" ]]; then
        # Linux: use free command or /proc/meminfo
        if command -v free &> /dev/null; then
            local ram_kb
            ram_kb=$(free -k | awk '/Mem:/ {print $2}')
            total_ram_gb=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $ram_kb/1024/1024}")
        elif [[ -f /proc/meminfo ]]; then
            local ram_kb
            ram_kb=$(grep MemTotal /proc/meminfo | awk '{print $2}')
            total_ram_gb=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $ram_kb/1024/1024}")
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "$total_ram_gb"
}

# Get disk usage percentage for root filesystem
get_disk_usage() {
    local usage_percent="Unknown"
    
    if command -v df &> /dev/null; then
        # Get percentage used for root filesystem
        usage_percent=$(df / 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR==2 {print $5}' | sed 's/%//' || echo "Unknown")
    fi
    
    echo "$usage_percent"
}

# Main execution
main() {
    local os
    os=$(detect_os)
    
    if [[ "$os" == "unknown" ]]; then
        echo "Error: Unable to detect operating system" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    
    local cpu_model
    local total_ram
    local disk_usage
    
    cpu_model=$(get_cpu_model "$os")
    total_ram=$(get_total_ram "$os")
    disk_usage=$(get_disk_usage)
    
    echo "CPU: $cpu_model"
    echo "RAM: ${total_ram} GB"
    echo "Disk: ${disk_usage}% used"
}

main "$@"

apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization

# Base namespace
namespace: production

# Metadata common to all resources
commonLabels:
  app.kubernetes.io/part-of: openclaw
  app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: kustomize

# Resources to deploy
resources:
  - namespace.yaml
  - configmap.yaml
  - secret.yaml
  - postgres-deployment.yaml
  - redis-deployment.yaml
  - auth-service-deployment.yaml
  - core-service-deployment.yaml
  - api-gateway-deployment.yaml
  - ingress.yaml

# ConfigMap generator for environment-specific configs
configMapGenerator:
  - name: postgres-init-scripts
    namespace: production
    options:
      disableNameSuffixHash: true
    literals:
      - init.sql=|
          CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
          CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pg_stat_statements";

# Secret generator (use external-secrets operator in production)
secretGenerator:
  - name: tls-certs
    namespace: production
    options:
      disableNameSuffixHash: true
    # Note: In production, use external-secrets or cert-manager

# Images to replace in deployments
images:
  - name: openclaw/api-gateway
    newTag: v1.2.3
  - name: openclaw/auth-service
    newTag: v1.2.3
  - name: openclaw/core-service
    newTag: v1.2.3

# Patches for production-specific modifications
patches:
  - target:
      kind: Deployment
      name: api-gateway
    patch: |
      - op: replace
        path: /spec/replicas
        value: 5
  - target:
      kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
      name: api-gateway-hpa
    patch: |
      - op: replace
        path: /spec/maxReplicas
        value: 20

# Replacements for cross-resource references
replacements:
  - source:
      kind: ConfigMap
      name: app-config
      fieldPath: data.DB_HOST
    targets:
      - select:
          kind: Deployment
          name: auth-service
        fieldPaths:
          - spec.template.spec.containers.[name=auth-service].env.[name=DATABASE_HOST].value
